name: Clinch-Powell Resource Conservation And Development Council, Inc
description_short: HUD-approved non-profit housing counselor and community development organization serving Tennessee since 1989, offering financial education, housing solutions, and conservation programs.
description_long:
Clinch-Powell Resource Conservation and Development Council has operated as a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization since 1989, focusing on sustainable community development across Tennessee. As a federally-designated Community Housing Development Organization (CHDO) and HUD-Approved Counseling Agency, the organization addresses three core mission areas: building strong communities, caring for people, and protecting natural resources.

The organization provides housing counseling to families, facilitates mortgage financing and home construction/rehabilitation, offers financial education programs, and supports small business development with affordable capital access. They also operate conservation initiatives working with farmers and provide community development support through partnerships and direct investments. Their service delivery includes both direct counseling and referral-based support for housing and financial matters.

Clinch-Powell distinguishes itself through its integrated approach combining housing assistance, conservation work, and community economic development in a rural Appalachian region. The organization's dual focus on housing solutions and environmental stewardship, along with youth engagement through service-learning projects, sets it apart from single-issue non-profit counselors. Their HUD approval and CHDO designation indicate compliance with federal counseling standards and housing program requirements.

As a free-help non-profit, Clinch-Powell is appropriate for individuals seeking legitimate housing counseling and financial guidance without commercial lending pressure. However, their service area appears concentrated in Tennessee, and specific details about counselor credentials, response times, and whether all services are truly free-of-charge are not explicitly stated on their website.
pros: HUD-Approved Counseling Agency status ensures compliance with federal housing counseling standards
Community Housing Development Organization (CHDO) designation demonstrates federally-recognized community housing expertise
501(c)(3) non-profit structure with 35+ years of operational history (since 1989) indicates organizational stability
Demonstrated impact: $17 million in mortgage financing facilitated and 322 families served through housing counseling
Integrated services combining housing counseling, mortgage assistance, and home rehabilitation in one organization
Equal opportunity provider with explicit non-discrimination policy addressing race, color, national origin, religion, sex, disability, and age
Regional expertise in rural Appalachian community development with conservation and small business support
cons: Service area appears limited to Tennessee; no indication of national reach or remote counseling options
Website does not explicitly state whether all counseling services are free or if fees apply for certain programs
Limited transparency about counselor qualifications, certifications beyond HUD approval, or staff credentials
No information provided about average response times, wait lists, or how quickly clients can access counseling services
Website contains no client testimonials, satisfaction ratings, or outcome metrics beyond aggregate impact statistics
best_for: Tennessee residents seeking HUD-approved housing counseling and mortgage guidance
Low-to-moderate income families needing affordable housing solutions and rehabilitation support
Individuals interested in community development-oriented financial education with conservation focus
diagnosis: Clinch-Powell is best for Tennessee residents seeking legitimate, federally-approved non-profit housing counseling and community development support. The main caveat is that their service area appears geographically limited to Tennessee, and prospective clients should contact them directly to confirm service eligibility, fee structures, and current availability.
services: Affordable capital and small business support
Vendor market and community events coordination
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
